title: Portuguese Prune Stew
description: A dense and briny stew featuring prunes and lacquer glaze, perfect for a low carb diet and a sick day, this traditional Portuguese dish is sure to comfort and satisfy.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional 1 minute.
Add the pork shoulder and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the chicken broth, prunes, lacquer glaze, salt, and pepper, and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 30 minutes.
Remove the lid and continue to simmer for an additional 10 minutes, or until the meat is tender.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
Cook pork to an internal temperature of 63°C / 145°F and let it rest for 3 minutes before serving.
